svg.text: Ceph Architecture: clients → RADOS daemons → per-OSD BlueStore stack
Layer 1 — Clients (user-space libraries / S3 callers, talk directly to OSDs after MON map fetch)
Layer 2 — RADOS daemons (cluster-wide, deployed by cephadm / rook)
Layer 3 — Per-OSD storage internals (one stack per ceph-osd process, typical one OSD per disk)
RADOS object → (data extent on raw block) + (omap / xattr / metadata in RocksDB)
deferred writes / WAL / checksum / compression / mempools — src/os/bluestore/BlueStore.cc
user-space FS just for RocksDB files
shares the block device with BlueStore
Linux kernel block layer (no filesystem; OSD opens device with O_DIRECT)
kernel does NOT see RADOS objects — just raw block IO from the OSD process
historic FileStore (XFS) is deprecated; v19.2.3 default is BlueStore on raw device
cgroup / IO scheduler still apply (per-OSD systemd unit)
Source: ceph v19.2.3 src/{ceph_mon, ceph_mgr, ceph_osd, mon/Monitor, os/bluestore, blk/kernel/KernelDevice}
